data.narrative:
Rocket Factory Augsburg is a Series B-stage space & aerospace company that has raised $80M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 5 public-market peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies, with direct peer benchmarks including Aalyria ($1.3B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $80M total raised as a proxy valuation floor, implying $152M. The peer-median approach across three same-sector, same-stage companies yields $400M, with the distribution spanning $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied absent disclosed revenue figures. These two methods produce a low of $129M, a median of $276M, and a high of $460M.

The $276M midpoint reflects material uncertainty across the peer set. The $187M spread between stage-progression and peer-median suggests either that Rocket Factory trades at a discount to comparable private-stage funding or that peer valuations contain wide dispersion tied to differentiated technology, customer traction, or capital efficiency. Key unknowns include near-term revenue trajectory, customer concentration, and launch cadence relative to stated capability. A valuation in the $200M–$350M range appears defensible pending clarity on commercial revenue.
